rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. NEW LITE MARKET, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

THE CONTRACT IN THE COURT'S FILE IS ILLEGABLE. IT ALSO APPEARS THAT THE PARTIES AGREE THE LIQUOR HAD TO BE DELIVERED TO 359 DIVISADERO STREET. THE INVOICES REFLECT SUCH A DELIVERY. DEFENDANTS' DISPUTE THE FACT AND PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED RYAN'S DECLARATION IN REPLY BUT THAT DOES NOT ESTABLISH WHERE THE LIQUOR WAS DELIVERED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: KEARNEY STREET LAW OFFICES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. JOHN ARTHUR SEBASTINELLI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion To Strike And Special Motion To Strike Pusuant To Cco 425.16 And Request For Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $5,475.00;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 20, 2007, LINE 7, CROSS COMPLAINANT JOHN SEBASTINELLI, PATRICIA SEBASTINELLI'S Notice Of Special Motion To Strike And Special Motion To Strike Pusuant To Cco 425.16 And Request For Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $5,475.00; THE MOTION IS DENIED. IN TRADE SECRET CASES, IT IS REASONABLE TO EXPECT THAT THE NEW EMPLOYER WILL BE A DEFENDANT. ONE INTERPRETATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CONFLICT IS THAT PLAINTIFF PROCEEDED WITH CAUTION IN NAMING M & R, WAITING UNTIL DISCOVERY SUPPORTED PALINTIFF'S POSITION. THE OTHER ALTERNATIVE IS TO ACCEPT M & R'S FACTUAL SCENARIO, IN PARTICULAR THE AGUILAR E-MAIL OF SEPT. 6, 2006. ASSUMING M & R HAS SHIFTED THE BURDEN, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF, IN FACT, CAN PROVE ITS CASE AGAINST M & R. ALTHOUGH PORTIONS OF THE AGUILAR DECLARATION CONSIST OF ARGUMENT AND ARE ADMISSABLE, THE INFERENCES LINKING THE EVIDENCE CONSTITUTE APPROPRIATE ARGUMENT FOR THE COURT TO CONSIDER IN EVALUATION THE ABILITY OF PLAINTIFF TO PROVE ITS CASE. FEES IN THE SUM OF $,5000 ARE AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LARRY EDER VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND GAME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Or Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 20, 2007, LINE 14, PETITIONER ROBERT EDER, JUSTIN YAGER, BRYAN RANDALL'S Ntc Of Mtn For Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus Or Writ Of Mandate. The Petition for Writ of Mandate is denied. The parties agree that the record is undisputed and the Court reviews the issues de novo. Petitioner seeks to declare California Fish & Game § 8279.1(c) unconstitutional and the act of revoking Petitioner's permit to fish in California unconstitutional. Section 6063, passed by Congress in 2005, moots Petitioner's challenge under the Commerce Clause. Schutz v. Thorne (10th Cir.) 415 F.3d 1128, 1130, 1137-38 makes clear that when Congress acts, the dormancy ends and courts are obliged to follow congressional will. In Section 6063, Congress made clear that each State may continue to regulate the taking of fish and may differentiate between residents and nonresidents. Moreover, under a traditional Commerce Clause analysis, Section 8279.1(c) would not be unconstitutional. The regulation applies equally to California residents and residents of other states and California has important interests in regulating the taking of crab. The statute is a rationale way to address the "race for crabs" that threatens the crab supply in California. It simply provides that if a one fishes early in California and there is a delay in Oregon and Washington, the fisher must delay. One can readily discern that the objective and rationale purpose of this statute is to ensure that the crab population of California is not depleted by excessive fishing as a consequence of a delay in the opening of the crab season in Oregon and Washington. California is not regulating conduct in Oregon; rather, it is setting forth reasonable conditions to fish in California for the long-term protection of California crab. To the extent there is any burden on interstate commerce or Petitioner, it is minimal. Petitioner's reliance on Nielsen v. Oregon (1909) 212 U.S.
